front: What is Continuous Internal Evaluation (CIE) in machine learning education?
back: An assessment method where students are evaluated throughout the course rather than through a single final exam, focusing on regular assignments, projects, and quizzes

front: Name three common components of CIE in machine learning courses
back: Programming assignments, theory quizzes, mini-projects/implementations, research paper reviews, and peer evaluations

front: Why is CIE particularly important for machine learning courses?
back: Because ML requires both theoretical understanding and practical implementation skills, which are better assessed through continuous evaluation rather than one-time exams

front: What is the typical weightage distribution for CIE versus end-semester exams in ML courses?
back: Usually 40-50% for CIE components and 50-60% for final examinations, though this varies by institution

front: How does CIE help in better learning outcomes for machine learning?
back: It encourages regular study habits, provides immediate feedback, allows for course correction, and assesses both theoretical concepts and practical coding skills
